revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「 {{Infobox 山 |名称 = 天狗山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市羽黒町より臨む |標高 = 493 |…」
revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 天狗山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市羽黒町より臨む |標高 = 493 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[山梨県]][[甲府市]] |山系 = [[奥秩父山塊]] |種類 = [[山塊]] |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Embedmap|138.5495|35.6949|300}}湯村山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|天狗山|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|天狗山}} }} '''天狗山'''（てんぐやま）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にあり、[[奥秩父山塊|奥秩父山地]]の南端部に位置する[[山]]である。[[高さ#地理|標高]]は493[[メートル]]。[[甲府名山]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == 山梨県甲府市羽黒町に位置し、山頂からは[[甲府盆地]]を一望することができる。 麓にある羽黒大宮神社の御神体とされ、ここから登山道を経て山頂に至る。登山道はここから更に[[千代田湖]]方面へと続いている。 山頂には[[古墳]]があり、直径約30m・高さ約6mの[[積石塚]]の[[円墳]]で、積石塚としては県内最大規模である[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html]。 墳丘上や周辺には神社や祠、石碑や石柱が存在する。 == 隣接する山 == * [[片山]] == 関連項目 == *[[奥秩父山塊]] *[[甲府名山]] *[[千代田湖]] *[[積石塚]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献 == 山梨県『山梨県史』資料編1 原始・古代1 1998年3月 == 外部リンク == *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html 甲府市公式サイト内　開府500年コラム 地名の由来6～「羽黒・中村」～] {{Mountain-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんぐやま}} [[Category:アジアの山]] [[Category:山梨県の山]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]]

{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 天狗山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市羽黒町より臨む（2021年1月撮影） |標高 = 493 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[山梨県]][[甲府市]] |山系 = [[奥秩父山塊]] |種類 = [[山塊]] |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}天狗山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|天狗山|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|天狗山}} }} '''天狗山'''（てんぐやま）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にあり、[[奥秩父山塊|奥秩父山地]]の南端部に位置する[[山]]である。[[高さ#地理|標高]]は493[[メートル]]。[[甲府名山]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == 山梨県甲府市羽黒町に位置し、山頂からは[[甲府盆地]]を一望することができる。麓にある羽黒大宮神社の御神体とされ、ここから登山道を経て山頂に至る。登山道はここから更に[[千代田湖]]方面へと続いている。 山頂にはおてんぐさん古墳（天狗山古墳）があり、直径約30m・高さ約6mの[[積石塚]]の[[円墳]]で、積石塚としては県内最大規模である[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html]。墳丘上や周辺には神社や祠、石碑や石柱が存在する。 == 隣接する山 == * [[片山 (山梨県)|片山]] == 関連項目 == *[[奥秩父山塊]] *[[甲府名山]] *[[千代田湖]] *[[積石塚]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献 == 山梨県『山梨県史』資料編1 原始・古代1 1998年3月 == 外部リンク == *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html 甲府市公式サイト内　開府500年コラム 地名の由来6～「羽黒・中村」～] {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=おてんぐさん古墳 |1-1=山梨県の古墳|1-2=甲府市の歴史|1-3=円墳|1-4=積石塚 }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんぐやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:山梨県の山]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]] [[Category:奥秩父の山]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Burial Mound (Mount Tengu Burial Mound), a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Burial Mound |1-1=Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular burial mounds|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:Mountains of the Okuchichibu Range]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Tengu]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Tengu]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Tengu]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Mount Tengu]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Tengu]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Mount Tengu]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Mount Tengu]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Mount Tengu]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%8B%97%E5%B1%B1 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天狗山]]

{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Burial Mound (Mount Tengu Burial Mound), a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okuchichibu {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Burial Mound (Mount Tengu Burial Mound), a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Burial Mound (Mount Tengu Burial Mound), a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Burial Mound |1-1=Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Burial Mound (Mount Tengu Burial Mound), a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Maqqlot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Burial Mound (Mount Tengu Burial Mound), a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} with a stone mound aqqroximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Maqqlot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Burial Mound (Mount Tengu Burial Mound), a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} with a stone mound aqqroximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Maqqlot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} with a stone mound aqqroximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} with a stone mound [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} with a stone mound [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}}== See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} with a stone mound [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 天狗山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市羽黒町より臨む（2021年1月撮影） |標高 = 493 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[山梨県]][[甲府市]] |山系 = [[奥秩父山塊]] |種類 = [[山塊]] |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}天狗山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|天狗山|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|天狗山}} }} '''天狗山'''（てんぐやま）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にあり、[[奥秩父山塊|奥秩父山地]]の南端部に位置する[[山]]である。[[高さ#地理|標高]]は493[[メートル]]。[[甲府名山]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == 山梨県甲府市羽黒町に位置し、山頂からは[[甲府盆地]]を一望することができる。麓にある羽黒大宮神社の御神体とされ、ここから登山道を経て山頂に至る。登山道はここから更に[[千代田湖]]方面へと続いている。 山頂にはおてんぐさん古墳（天狗山古墳）があり、直径約30m・高さ約6mの[[積石塚]]の[[円墳]]で、積石塚としては県内最大規模である[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html]。墳丘上や周辺には神社や祠、石碑や石柱が存在する。 == 隣接する山 == * [[片山 (山梨県)|片山]] == 関連項目 == *[[奥秩父山塊]] *[[甲府名山]] *[[千代田湖]] *[[積石塚]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献 == 山梨県『山梨県史』資料編1 原始・古代1 1998年3月 == 外部リンク == *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html 甲府市公式サイト内　開府500年コラム 地名の由来6～「羽黒・中村」～] {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=おてんぐさん古墳 |1-1=山梨県の古墳|1-2=甲府市の歴史|1-3=円墳|1-4=積石塚 }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんぐやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:山梨県の山]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]] [[Category:奥秩父の山]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 天狗山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市羽黒町より臨む（2021年1月撮影） |標高 = 493 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[山梨県]][[甲府市]] |山系 = [[奥秩父山塊]] |種類 = [[山塊]] |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}天狗山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|天狗山|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|天狗山}} }} '''天狗山'''（てんぐやま）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にあり、[[奥秩父山塊|奥秩父山地]]の南端部に位置する[[山]]である。[[高さ#地理|標高]]は493[[メートル]]。[[甲府名山]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == 山梨県甲府市羽黒町に位置し、山頂からは[[甲府盆地]]を一望することができる。麓にある羽黒大宮神社の御神体とされ、ここから登山道を経て山頂に至る。登山道はここから更に[[千代田湖]]方面へと続いている。 山頂にはおてんぐさん古墳（天狗山古墳）があり、直径約30m・高さ約6mの[[積石塚]]の[[円墳]]で、積石塚としては県内最大規模である[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html]。墳丘上や周辺には神社や祠、石碑や石柱が存在する。 == 隣接する山 == * [[片山 (山梨県)|片山]] == 関連項目 == *[[奥秩父山塊]] *[[甲府名山]] *[[千代田湖]] *[[積石塚]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献 == 山梨県『山梨県史』資料編1 原始・古代1 1998年3月 == 外部リンク == *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html 甲府市公式サイト内　開府500年コラム 地名の由来6～「羽黒・中村」～] {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=おてんぐさん古墳 |1-1=山梨県の古墳|1-2=甲府市の歴史|1-3=円墳|1-4=積石塚 }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんぐやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:山梨県の山]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]] [[Category:奥秩父の山]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] | Range = [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|qq=}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|qq=}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|qq=}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|qq=}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone mound burial site|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular Kofuns|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|天狗山 (甲府市)|version=92560537|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:Mountains of the Okuchichibu Range]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Mount Tengu]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%8B%97%E5%B1%B1 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天狗山]] [[sn:ount Tengu]] [[Category:奥秩父の山]] [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[ja:天狗山 (甲府市)]]

It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|qq=}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|qq=}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone mound burial site|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular Kofuns|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|天狗山 (甲府市)|version=92560537|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:Mountains of the Okuchichibu Range]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Mount Tengu]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%8B%97%E5%B1%B1 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天狗山]] [[sn:ount Tengu]] [[Category:奥秩父の山]] [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[ja:天狗山 (甲府市)]]

It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone mound burial site|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular Kofuns|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|天狗山 (甲府市)|version=92560537|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天狗山]] [[Category:Mountains of Oku-Chichibu]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[ja:天狗山 (甲府市)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442932}}

It is situated at the southern edge of the [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone mound burial site|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular Kofuns|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|天狗山 (甲府市)|version=92560537|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:天狗山]] [[ja:天狗山 (甲府市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442932}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Oku-Chichibu]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone mound burial site|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular Kofuns|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|天狗山 (甲府市)|version=92560537|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天狗山]] [[ja:天狗山 (甲府市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442932}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Oku-Chichibu]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Qq]]

[[ja:天狗山]][[zh-yue:天狗山]] [[ja:天狗山]][[zh-yue:天狗山]] [[ja:天狗山]][[zh-yue:天狗山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442932]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:22, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Tengu | Photo = [[File:Mt.Tengu 210130.jpg|300px|]] | Photo_caption = View from Kofu City, Haguro Town (Photo taken January 2021) | Elevation_m = 493 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|41|N|138|32|58|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] | Range = [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone mound burial site|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular Kofuns|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|天狗山 (甲府市)|version=92560537|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天狗山]] [[ja:天狗山 (甲府市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442932}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Oku-Chichibu]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone mound burial site|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular Kofuns|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|天狗山 (甲府市)|version=92560537|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天狗山]] [[ja:天狗山 (甲府市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442932}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Oku-Chichibu]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}} | Range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains|1=Okuchichibu Mountains}} | Type = Mountain block | Map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6949|138.5495}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Mount Tengu{{日本の位置情報|35|41|41|138|32|58|Mount Tengu|35.694988921712884, 138.54956125753014|Mount Tengu}} }} '''Mount Tengu''' (天狗山, Tengu-yama) is a mountain located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is situated at the southern edge of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains|1=Okuchichibu Mountains}} and has an elevation of 493 meters. It is recognized as one of the {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Mount Tengu is located in Haguro Town, Kofu City, and offers panoramic views of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}} from its summit. The mountain serves as the sacred body of the nearby Haguro Ōmiya Shrine. A trail from the shrine leads to the summit and continues toward {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}}. At the summit lies the O-Tengu-san Kofun (Mount Tengu Kofun), a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a stone mound approximately 30 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It is the largest stone mound burial site in Yamanashi Prefecture.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names] The area is dotted with shrines, altars, stone monuments, and pillars. == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja|片山 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama (Yamanashi Prefecture)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains|1=Okuchichibu Mountains}} * {{ill|Famous Mountains of Kofu|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Mountains of Kofu|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|stone mound burial site|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone mound burial site|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yamanashi Prefecture, ''Yamanashi Prefectural History: Historical Records 1 - Prehistoric and Ancient 1,'' March 1998. == External Links == * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/koho/kaifukoramu/timei06.html Kofu City Official Site - Column on the Origin of Place Names: "Haguro and Nakamura"] {{Redirect category |redirect1=O-Tengu-san Kofun |1-1=Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture|1-2=History of Kofu|1-3=Circular Kofuns|1-4=Stone mound burial sites }} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|天狗山 (甲府市)|version=92560537|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天狗山]] [[ja:天狗山 (甲府市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442932}} [[Category:Lists of mountains]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Oku-Chichibu]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
